headline: Morgan Stanley stays bullish on aerospace, defense ahead of Q2 earnings
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley maintained a constructive outlook on the aerospace and defense sector ahead of second-quarter earnings, citing resilient commercial aerospace demand, improving aircraft production, and favorable long-term defense spending trends, while becoming more selective after recent stock volatility and valuation shifts.

headline: SpaceX Stock Is in a Bear Market. This Can Turn It Around.
summary: SpaceX  stock is dangerously close to breaking its $135 IPO price.  SpaceX is set to test its huge, fully-reusable Starship for the thirteenth time.  Starship promises to dramatically lower the cost of reaching space while dramatically increasing SpaceX’s capacity to put things into orbit.

headline: Walden Robotics Launches with $300 Million to Put General-Purpose Robots to Work Today
summary: CAMBRIDGE, Mass., July 15, 2026--Walden Robotics, a full-stack Physical AI company building and deploying general-purpose robots that continuously learn and improve while performing real work, launched out of stealth with $300 million in funding. The round, which values the company at $1.1 billion, is co-led by Toyota (Toyota Motor Corp, Toyota Invention Partners, and Toyota Ventures) and Deviation Capital, with participation from NVIDIA, Boeing, AE Ventures, Samsung Ventures, Prologis Ventures,

headline: Why Boeing (BA) Is Down 6.3% After Fresh 777X Delays And Supply Snags – And What's Next
summary: Boeing recently came under renewed regulatory scrutiny that could push 777X certification into the late 2020s, while ongoing supply chain bottlenecks are constraining 737 MAX production and weighing on operations. At the same time, Boeing’s presence at the Farnborough Airshow with its MQ-28 Ghost Bat and 777X displays highlights its efforts to restore confidence and tap emerging autonomous defense opportunities despite these operational headwinds. We’ll now explore how renewed regulatory...
